name: Pipeline Loop Line Sizing
expected_response_detailed: Loop line design: parallel segment reduces flow resistance. Loop line length fraction determined by required flow increase. Hydraulic calculation: equivalent resistance of parallel lines lower than either alone. Sizing: typically equal diameter to mainline, shorter length to achieve 10-20% capacity increase. Pig trap sizing: accommodate both main and loop. Valve arrangement: bypass to isolate either line. Tie-in: hot tap or shutdown. Economic: cost of loop vs compressor station.

name: Hydraulic Analysis Software
expected_response_detailed: Pipeline simulation: steady-state for capacity rating, transient for line pack and surge analysis, optimization for compressor scheduling. Common software: PipelineStudio (SPS), OLGA for multiphase, PIPESYS. Data requirements: pipe segments with elevation profile, diameter, wall, roughness, stations (compressors, heaters, valves), boundary conditions, fluid composition. Calibration: field data matching (pressure, flow, temperature). Sensitivity: what-if scenarios for operations.

name: Pressure Regulation Station
expected_response_detailed: Pressure regulation: monitor regulators (active/standby), pilot-operated valves, slam-shuts on high/low pressure. Sizing: Cv calculation for maximum flow at minimum inlet. Turndown: 10:1 typical, wider with dual-range. Accuracy: ±1% of setpoint. Response time: 1-2 seconds. Filter upstream: 40 micron typical. Relief protection downstream. Station piping: minimum bends, supports for weight, thermal expansion loops. Emergency response: full close time from control signal. SCADA: pressure trending, alarms on deviation.

name: Pipeline Recommissioning
expected_response_detailed: Recommissioning after mothball: inspection prior to return (external, ILI if long outage). Integrity verification: pressure test per original design or 192.619. Cleaning: pigging to remove deposits, dehydration if required. Purging: air removal for gas, inert gas (nitrogen) displacement. Valves: cycle testing, seat tightness. Instruments: calibration. Corrosion: inhibitor injection if internal. Cathodic protection: current requirement verification. Documentation: recommissioning checklist, as-left configurations.

name: Seismic Design Criteria
expected_response_detailed: ASCE 7 seismic design: spectral acceleration Ss and S1 from USGS maps. Site class (A-F) from soil properties. Design spectrum: short period and 1-second acceleration. Pipeline strain: longitudinal from wave propagation, transverse from PGV. Design: fault crossing accommodation (flexible joints, offset), liquefaction mitigation (deep burial, soil improvement), landslide avoidance or stabilization. ASME B31.8 Case N-755 for seismic. Emergency shutdown on seismic trigger (earthquake early warning).

name: Meter Proving
expected_response_detailed: Meter prover: pipe prover (displacement) or compact prover (piston). Prover volume: 10,000× meter resolution minimum for 0.05% uncertainty. Master meter method as alternative. Temperature compensation: 1°F = 0.3% volume change. Pressure: compressibility factor. Runs: minimum 5 consecutive within 0.05% repeatability. Frequency: annually or per contract. Factor calculation: prover volume / meter indicated. Uncertainty: 0.1% typical for pipe prover.

name: Pipeline Construction Inspection
expected_response_detailed: Construction inspection phases: clearing and grading, trenching, stringing, welding (100% of welds inspected X-ray or UT), joint coating, lowering in, backfill, testing, cleanup. Welding: WPS compliance, NDE acceptance, repair procedures. Coating: holiday testing 100%. Lowering: stress analysis, bending radius, sling protection. Backfill: material selection, compaction density. Test: hydrostatic, leak detection, pressure holding. Documentation: daily reports, as-built drawings, test records.

name: Pipeline Capacity Assessment
expected_response_detailed: Maximum operating capacity not always equal to nameplate: MAOP limit, compressor station power, temperature constraints, customer nominations. Capacity model: actual vs theoretical. Bottleneck identification: station with highest pressure ratio, pipeline segment with most friction. Debottlenecking: loop lines, compressor upgrades, drag reducers, MAOP increase. Seasonal capacity: winter demand peaks. Interruptible vs firm service. Storage injection/withdrawal impact on capacity booking.

name: Ground Bed Anode Design
expected_response_detailed: Deep anode bed: 100+ feet depth for low resistance, multiple tubular anodes in coke breeze backfill. Design: 50 Amp typical output, resistance less than 0.5 Ohm, 20-year design life. Anode consumption: 1 lb/Amp-year. Cable sizing: voltage drop calculation, HMWPE insulation rated for chlorine if applicable. Vent pipes for gas release. Monitoring: individual anode current measurement. Shallow ground bed: 20-50 feet spacing, graphite or HSCI anodes, horizontal or vertical. Soil resistivity testing for location.

name: Pipeline Abandonment
expected_response_detailed: Abandonment per 192.727: purge of hazardous liquids or gas (inert gas displacement), fill with water or inert material if potential contamination. Below grade discontinuation: cut and cap, or pull. Cathodic protection: disconnect and document. Environmental: remove if surface or water crossing, address contamination. Records: location remains in database. Notification: landowners, state agencies (3-6 months advance), PHMSA if hazardous line. Easement: review status, some require pipe removal per agreement.
